title: Epic 1.1 — Snapshot Strategy
description:
Verify avt API surface, implement the terminal state snapshot mechanism using `Vt::dump()`/restore, and define the core types used by the keyframe index.

## Context

`avt::Vt` does **not** implement `Clone`. However, `Vt::dump()` serializes the full terminal state (both screen buffers, cursor, pen, modes, charsets, tab stops, scroll margins, saved cursor contexts) into an escape sequence string. Feeding this dump into a fresh `Vt` of the **same dimensions** reproduces the exact state. This is the chosen snapshot strategy.

### Key constraints

- `dump()` does NOT capture terminal dimensions — the caller must track and restore these.
- When primary buffer is active, alternate buffer **content** is not captured (only saved cursor context). This is correct: entering alternate mode always starts a fresh buffer.
- `Vt::new()` creates a non-resizable Vt. All Vts for playback must use `Vt::builder().size(w, h).resizable(true).scrollback_limit(0).build()`.
- `avt::Cursor` is not publicly importable — we define our own `CursorState` type.

## Scope

- `crates/speedrun-core/src/snapshot.rs` (new)
- Updates to `lib.rs` and `Cargo.toml` (add `rgb = "0.8"` dependency)

## Tickets

- 1.1.1 — Snapshot types, capture, and restore
- 1.1.2 — Snapshot tests

title: 1.1.1 — Snapshot types, capture, and restore
notes:
## Codebase context

**Current state of files you will modify:**

- `crates/speedrun-core/src/lib.rs` contains only: `// speedrun-core library - Terminal session player core engine` (1 line, no modules).
- `crates/speedrun-core/Cargo.toml` has deps `avt.workspace = true`, `serde.workspace = true`, `serde_json.workspace = true`. Dev-deps: `insta`, `tempfile`, `pretty_assertions` (all `.workspace = true`).
- Root `Cargo.toml` has `[workspace.dependencies]` with `avt = "0.11"`, `serde`, `serde_json`, etc. Add `rgb = "0.8"` in the same section.
- `snapshot.rs` does not exist yet — create it.

**Other epics may modify `lib.rs` concurrently.** Just add your `pub mod snapshot;` line and re-exports. Do not assume the file is empty or that you own it exclusively.

**Adding the `rgb` dependency — exact syntax:**
- Root `Cargo.toml`: add `rgb = "0.8"` under `[workspace.dependencies]`
- `crates/speedrun-core/Cargo.toml`: add `rgb.workspace = true` under `[dependencies]`

## avt API quick reference

Key methods you will use (avt 0.11):

```
Vt::builder() -> Builder
Builder::size(cols: usize, rows: usize) -> &mut Builder
Builder::resizable(bool) -> &mut Builder
Builder::scrollback_limit(limit: usize) -> &mut Builder
Builder::build() -> Vt

vt.feed_str(s: &str) -> (Vec<usize>, bool)  // (dirty_lines, was_resized)
vt.dump() -> String          // serializes full state as escape sequences
vt.size() -> (usize, usize)  // (cols, rows) — NOTE: returns usize, not u16
vt.cursor() -> Cursor         // Cursor { pub col: usize, pub row: usize, pub visible: bool }
vt.view() -> &[Line]          // visible screen rows (exactly `rows` lines)
```

**Important**: `vt.size()` returns `(usize, usize)`. The snapshot stores `(u16, u16)`. You need an explicit cast: `width: size.0 as u16`.

## Verification commands

Run these before marking done:
```bash
cargo build -p speedrun-core
cargo clippy -p speedrun-core -- -D warnings
cargo fmt --check
```

title: 1.1.2 — Snapshot tests
description:
Comprehensive tests for snapshot capture/restore round-trips and cursor state extraction.

## Tests to implement

Add `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` to `snapshot.rs`:

1. **Basic round-trip**: Create Vt 10x4 via `create_vt(10, 4)`, feed `"hello\r\nworld"`, capture snapshot, restore to new Vt. Verify `view()` line text matches line-by-line (use `line.text()`). Verify `cursor()` col/row match between original and restored.

2. **Colors and attributes**: Feed escape sequences for bold (`\x1b[1m`), fg color (`\x1b[31m`), bg color (`\x1b[42m`), inverse (`\x1b[7m`). Write some text. Capture/restore. Verify attributes preserved by comparing `line.cells()` pen attributes (`is_bold()`, `foreground()`, etc.) on the restored Vt.

3. **Cursor state**: Test `CursorState::from_vt` across multiple scenarios in one test:
   - Default position (0, 0, visible) on a fresh Vt.
   - After feeding text to move cursor — verify col/row update.
   - Hide cursor (`\x1b[?25l`), capture/restore, verify `visible == false`.
   - Show cursor (`\x1b[?25h`), capture/restore, verify `visible == true`.

4. **Alternate buffer**: Enter alternate buffer (`\x1b[?1049h`), write text ("alt buffer content"), capture/restore, verify restored Vt shows the text via `view()`. Then in the same test or a follow-on assertion: exit alternate (`\x1b[?1049l`), write "primary content", capture/restore, verify primary buffer is correct. Add a comment explaining the asymmetry: alternate buffer content is intentionally not captured when primary is active — entering alternate always starts fresh.

5. **Resize**: Create Vt 80x24, feed resize escape sequence `\x1b[8;40;120t` (**note: xtwinops order is rows;cols**), write text. Capture snapshot. Assert `snapshot.width() == 120` and `snapshot.height() == 40`. Restore and verify `vt.size() == (120, 40)` and text content matches.

6. **Fresh Vt (empty recording edge case)**: Create Vt 80x24, capture immediately (no feed). Restore. Verify both are blank (all lines are empty/spaces) and cursors match (0, 0, visible).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `cargo test -p speedrun-core -- snapshot` passes
- [ ] All 6 test cases are implemented and pass
- [ ] Resize test uses correct xtwinops parameter order (rows;cols, not cols;rows)
- [ ] Alternate buffer asymmetry is documented with a comment in the test
- [ ] Fresh/empty Vt round-trip is tested
notes:
## avt API reference for tests

You will need these avt APIs to write assertions:

```
// Line text extraction
line.text() -> String              // plain text of a line

// Cell iteration (for attribute verification)
line.cells() -> impl Iterator<Item = (char, Pen)>

// Pen attribute getters
pen.foreground() -> Option<Color>  // Color::Indexed(u8) or Color::RGB(rgb::RGB8)
pen.background() -> Option<Color>
pen.is_bold() -> bool
pen.is_faint() -> bool
pen.is_italic() -> bool
pen.is_underline() -> bool
pen.is_strikethrough() -> bool
pen.is_blink() -> bool
pen.is_inverse() -> bool

// Color types
avt::Color::Indexed(u8)
avt::Color::RGB(rgb::RGB8)    // rgb::RGB8 has pub fields r, g, b
```

**Comparing restored state**: The simplest approach is to compare line text (`line.text()`) for content and spot-check specific cells for attributes. Do NOT try to compare `Line` directly (it has private fields).

**The testdata_path helper** is mentioned for convention only — this ticket builds all Vts in-memory. Do not import testdata files.

## Verification commands

Run these before marking done:
```bash
cargo test -p speedrun-core -- snapshot
cargo clippy -p speedrun-core -- -D warnings
cargo fmt --check
```

title: Epic 0 — Project Setup
description: Buildable, testable, linted workspace with CI-equivalent local checks, test data, tooling bootstrap (beads + pit), and agent onboarding guide.

title: 0.1 — Workspace scaffold
description:
Create the Cargo workspace with both crates, minimal lib.rs/main.rs stubs that compile, and workspace-level configuration.

## Tasks

- Root Cargo.toml: workspace with members `crates/speedrun-core` and `crates/speedrun`. Declare shared dependencies at workspace level using `[workspace.dependencies]` so both crates inherit versions.
- `crates/speedrun-core/Cargo.toml`: deps `avt`, `serde` (with derive feature), `serde_json`, inherited from workspace. Stub `src/lib.rs` exporting nothing (empty file or a comment).
- `crates/speedrun/Cargo.toml`: deps `speedrun-core` (path = "../speedrun-core"), `ratatui`, `crossterm`, `clap` (with derive feature), inherited from workspace. Stub `src/main.rs` that prints "speedrun: no file specified" and exits.
- Workspace dev-dependencies in `[workspace.dependencies]`: `insta`, `tempfile`, `pretty_assertions`.
- Rust edition 2024 set via `[workspace.package]` and inherited by both crates.
- Update `.gitignore`: ensure `/target` is listed (workspace-level build dir). Add `/.beads/*.db` for the beads database.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `cargo build --workspace` succeeds with zero warnings
- [ ] `cargo test --workspace` succeeds (even with no tests yet)
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` passes
- [ ] Both crates use edition 2024 (check via `cargo metadata`)
- [ ] `crates/speedrun-core/src/lib.rs` exists and compiles
- [ ] `crates/speedrun/src/main.rs` exists, compiles, and prints a message when run
- [ ] `.gitignore` includes `/target` and `/.beads/*.db`

title: 0.2 — Tooling and pre-commit hooks
description:
Set up formatting, linting, and pre-commit hooks so that CI-equivalent checks run locally before every commit.

## Tasks

- Create `rustfmt.toml` at workspace root with default config (empty file — use rustfmt defaults).
- Add `[workspace.lints.clippy]` to root `Cargo.toml` with `all = "deny"`. Both crates inherit via `[lints] workspace = true`.
- Create a pre-commit hook script at `scripts/install-hooks.sh` that symlinks `.git/hooks/pre-commit` to a `scripts/pre-commit` script. The pre-commit script runs, in order:
  1. `cargo fmt --check` — reject unformatted code
  2. `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` — reject lint warnings
  3. `cargo test --workspace` — reject failing tests
  If any step fails, the commit is aborted with a clear message indicating which check failed.
- The install script should be idempotent (safe to run multiple times).
- Document in a comment at the top of `install-hooks.sh`: run `bash scripts/install-hooks.sh` after cloning.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `rustfmt.toml` exists at workspace root
- [ ] `cargo fmt --check` passes on the scaffold
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` passes on the scaffold
- [ ] `scripts/pre-commit` exists and is executable
- [ ] `scripts/install-hooks.sh` exists and is executable
- [ ] After running `install-hooks.sh`, a commit with a fmt violation (e.g., extra spaces) is rejected by the hook
- [ ] After running `install-hooks.sh`, a commit with a clippy warning is rejected by the hook
- [ ] Running `install-hooks.sh` twice does not error or create duplicate hooks

title: 0.3 — Test data
description:
Create synthetic .cast files for testing. All files are hand-crafted, minimal, and human-readable. These are the fixtures that Phase 1 parser tests will validate against.

## Asciicast format reference

**V2 header:** First line is a JSON object. Required fields: `version` (int, must be 2), `width` (int), `height` (int). Optional: `timestamp` (unix epoch), `idle_time_limit` (float, seconds), `title` (string), `env` (object).
Example: `{"version": 2, "width": 80, "height": 24}`

**V2 events:** Subsequent lines are JSON arrays: `[timestamp, "event_type", "data"]`.
Timestamps are absolute seconds from start (float). Event types: `o` (output), `i` (input), `m` (marker with label as data), `r` (resize, data is `"COLSxROWS"`).
Example: `[0.5, "o", "hello\r\n"]`

**V3 header:** Same as V2 but `"version": 3`.
**V3 events:** Same array shape but timestamps are relative (interval since previous event, not absolute).

## Valid test files (testdata/)

- `minimal_v2.cast`: V2 header (80x24) + 3-5 output events with varying timestamps (e.g., 0.5s, 1.2s, 2.0s). Content: simple text like `$ hello\r\nworld\r\n`.
- `minimal_v3.cast`: V3 header (80x24) + 3-5 output events with relative timestamps (e.g., 0.5, 0.7, 0.8). Same kind of simple output.
- `empty.cast`: Valid V2 header, zero event lines.
- `with_markers.cast`: V2 header + mix of output and at least 2 marker (`m`) events with labels. E.g., `[3.0, "m", "chapter-1"]`, `[7.0, "m", "chapter-2"]`.
- `with_resize.cast`: V2 header (80x24) + output events + at least 1 resize (`r`) event. E.g., `[2.0, "r", "120x40"]`.
- `long_idle.cast`: V2 header with `"idle_time_limit": 2`. Events: output at 1.0s, then output at 35.0s (34-second gap), then output at 36.0s. This tests idle capping.
- `alternate_buffer.cast`: V2 header + output events that switch to alternate screen buffer and back. Use these exact sequences:
  - Enter alt buffer: `\u001b[?1049h`
  - Write something in alt buffer
  - Exit alt buffer: `\u001b[?1049l`
- `real_session.cast`: A realistic recording, 30+ seconds long. Hand-craft ~20 output events over 30s simulating a shell session (commands, output, prompts with realistic timing gaps).

## Invalid test files (testdata/invalid/)

- `empty_file.cast`: Exactly zero bytes.
- `no_header.cast`: Event lines only, no JSON header on line 1. E.g., `[0.5, "o", "hello"]` as first line.
- `bad_json.cast`: Invalid JSON on line 1. E.g., `{"version": 2, "width": 80, "height": 24` (missing closing brace).
- `bad_version.cast`: Valid JSON header but `"version": 99`.
- `missing_fields.cast`: Valid JSON header but missing `width` and `height`. E.g., `{"version": 2}`.
- `bad_event.cast`: Valid header followed by malformed events: `[0.5, "o"]` (missing data), `not json at all`, `[null, "o", "x"]` (null timestamp).
- `binary_garbage.cast`: 64 bytes of random binary content (non-UTF8).
- `truncated.cast`: Valid header + one valid event + one incomplete line (e.g., `[1.5, "o", "hel`).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All valid .cast files exist in `testdata/`
- [ ] All invalid .cast files exist in `testdata/invalid/`
- [ ] Each valid file's first line is parseable JSON with the correct `version` field
- [ ] `long_idle.cast` header has `"idle_time_limit": 2`
- [ ] `with_markers.cast` contains at least 2 lines with `"m"` event type
- [ ] `with_resize.cast` contains at least 1 line with `"r"` event type
- [ ] `alternate_buffer.cast` contains the `1049h` and `1049l` escape sequences
- [ ] `empty_file.cast` is exactly 0 bytes
- [ ] `binary_garbage.cast` is not valid UTF-8
- [ ] File count: 8 valid + 8 invalid = 16 total .cast files

title: 0.4 — Populate AGENTS.md
description:
Populate the root AGENTS.md file with project-specific agent onboarding content. This is the file `pit init` created — it already contains pit orchestration instructions at the bottom. This ticket adds the project-specific sections above them.

This is a Human + Agent collaborative task. Do NOT autoclose.

## Sections to include

1. **Project overview** — speedrun is a terminal session player for asciicast (.cast) files with instant seeking and scrubbing. Two-crate Cargo workspace: `speedrun-core` (library, no UI) and `speedrun` (TUI binary).
2. **Build and test commands** — `cargo build --workspace`, `cargo test --workspace`, `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check`. Single crate: `cargo test -p speedrun-core`.
3. **Project structure** — Brief map of `crates/speedrun-core/src/` (lib.rs, parser.rs, timemap.rs, index.rs, player.rs, snapshot.rs) and `crates/speedrun/src/` (main.rs, app.rs, ui.rs, input.rs). What each file is responsible for.
4. **Issue tracking** — beads workflow: `bd ready`, `bd show <id>`, `bd update <id> --status in_progress`, `bd close <id>`, `bd sync`.
5. **Coding conventions** — Rust edition 2024, clippy deny warnings, rustfmt defaults. No `unwrap()` in library code (use typed errors). `insta` for snapshot tests. Workspace-level dependency declarations.
6. **Key dependencies** — `avt` (terminal emulation), `ratatui`/`crossterm` (TUI), `clap` (CLI), `serde`/`serde_json` (asciicast parsing).

## Also

- Create `docs/agents.md` if it does not yet exist — a one-liner pointing to root AGENTS.md.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] AGENTS.md at repo root contains all sections listed above
- [ ] Build commands in the file are accurate and runnable
- [ ] pit orchestration instructions (injected by `pit init`) are preserved
- [ ] A new agent reading only AGENTS.md could orient themselves and start working on a ticket
- [ ] Human has reviewed and approved the content

title: Epic 1.3 — Time Mapper
description:
Apply idle time limits to produce effective timestamps for each event. Computed once at load time.

## Context

Recordings may contain long idle gaps (e.g., 30+ seconds of inactivity). The idle limit (from CLI flag, header, or none) caps these gaps to produce a compressed "effective" timeline. All public API surfaces (progress bar, seek, duration) use effective time.

### Key design decisions

- **Decoupled from parser types**: `TimeMap::build()` takes a `&[f64]` of raw timestamps, not `&[Event]`. The caller extracts timestamps.
- **Idle limit as `Option<f64>`**: `None` = no capping (effective = raw). No enum wrapper.
- **First event time preserved**: the gap between t=0 and the first event is NOT capped. Only inter-event gaps are subject to idle limiting.
- **Binary search for seeking**: `event_index_at(time)` returns `Option<usize>` — the last event at or before the given effective time. Returns `None` for empty maps or times before the first event.

## Scope

- `crates/speedrun-core/src/timemap.rs` (new)
- Updates to `lib.rs`

## Tickets

- 1.3.1 — TimeMap implementation
- 1.3.2 — TimeMap tests

title: 1.3.1 — TimeMap implementation
description:
Implement the time mapper that converts raw event timestamps to effective (idle-capped) timestamps.

## Cross-cutting conventions

- **Idle limit as `Option<f64>`**: `None` = no capping. No enum wrapper.
- **Decoupled from parser**: `TimeMap::build()` takes `&[f64]` (raw timestamps), NOT `&[Event]`. The caller extracts timestamps. This keeps the module independent for parallel development.
- **Derives**: `TimeMap`: `Debug`. `TimeMapError`: `Debug`.

## Tasks

1. Create `crates/speedrun-core/src/timemap.rs` with:

   ```rust
   /// Mapping from raw timestamps to effective (idle-capped) timestamps.
   /// Computed once at load time. One entry per event, parallel to the events vec.
   #[derive(Debug)]
   pub struct TimeMap {
       effective_times: Vec<f64>,
       duration: f64,
   }

   #[derive(Debug)]
   pub enum TimeMapError {
       /// Idle limit must be positive.
       InvalidIdleLimit(f64),
   }
   ```
   Implement `std::fmt::Display` and `std::error::Error` for `TimeMapError`.

2. **Construction**:
   ```rust
   impl TimeMap {
       /// Build a time map from raw event timestamps.
       ///
       /// `idle_limit`: if `Some(limit)`, inter-event gaps exceeding `limit`
       /// seconds are capped to `limit`. If `None`, effective = raw.
       pub fn build(raw_times: &[f64], idle_limit: Option<f64>) -> Result<Self, TimeMapError>
   }
   ```

3. **Algorithm**:
   - If `idle_limit` is `Some(limit)` and `limit <= 0.0` → return `Err(InvalidIdleLimit(limit))`.
   - If `raw_times` is empty → return `Ok(TimeMap { effective_times: vec![], duration: 0.0 })`.
   - `effective_times[0] = raw_times[0]`. The gap between t=0 and the first event is NOT capped — it is the recording's natural start offset.
   - For `i` in `1..len`:
     - `gap = raw_times[i] - raw_times[i-1]`
     - `capped_gap = match idle_limit { Some(limit) => gap.min(limit), None => gap }`
     - `effective_times[i] = effective_times[i-1] + capped_gap`
   - `duration = *effective_times.last().unwrap_or(&0.0)`

4. **Accessors**:
   ```rust
   /// Effective timestamp for event at index. None if out of bounds.
   pub fn effective_time(&self, index: usize) -> Option<f64>

   /// Total effective duration (effective time of last event, or 0.0 if empty).
   pub fn duration(&self) -> f64

   /// Number of entries (same as number of events).
   pub fn len(&self) -> usize
   pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool

   /// Find the last event at or before `time` in effective time.
   /// Returns None if the time map is empty or time is before the first event.
   pub fn event_index_at(&self, time: f64) -> Option<usize>
   ```

5. **`event_index_at` implementation**: Use `partition_point` for binary search on `effective_times`. Find the number of entries where `effective_times[i] <= time`, then return `that_count - 1` (the last entry at-or-before). If the count is 0 (time is before all entries), return `None`. If the vec is empty, return `None`.

   Note on f64 precision: the values produced by this algorithm are simple additions/subtractions of small numbers. No epsilon tolerance is needed — exact `<=` comparison is correct for the values we produce.

6. Add `pub mod timemap;` to `lib.rs`. Re-export: `pub use timemap::{TimeMap, TimeMapError};`

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `cargo build -p speedrun-core` passes with zero warnings
- [ ] `TimeMap::build` handles: no limit, with limit, empty input, invalid limit (<=0)
- [ ] First event time is preserved (not capped)
- [ ] Inter-event gaps exceeding the limit are capped; gaps at or below limit are untouched
- [ ] `event_index_at` returns `None` for empty maps and times before first event
- [ ] `event_index_at` returns correct index for exact matches, between-events, and past-end
- [ ] `TimeMapError` implements `Display` and `Error`
- [ ] Types are exported from `lib.rs`
notes:
## Codebase context

**Current state of files you will modify:**

- `crates/speedrun-core/src/lib.rs` contains only: `// speedrun-core library - Terminal session player core engine` (1 line, no modules).
- `crates/speedrun-core/Cargo.toml` already has all needed deps. No new dependencies required for this module.
- `timemap.rs` does not exist yet — create it.

**Other epics may modify `lib.rs` concurrently** (snapshot.rs and parser.rs are being built in parallel). Just add your `pub mod timemap;` line and re-exports. Do not assume the file is empty or that you own it exclusively.

**This module has no dependency on the parser module.** `TimeMap::build()` takes `&[f64]` (raw timestamps), not `&[Event]`. This is deliberate — it keeps the epics independent for parallel development.

## Verification commands

Run these before marking done:
```bash
cargo build -p speedrun-core
cargo clippy -p speedrun-core -- -D warnings
cargo fmt --check
```

title: 1.3.2 — TimeMap tests
description:
Tests for the time mapper. Tests use raw timestamp slices directly — no dependency on the parser module.

## Tests to implement

Add `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` to `timemap.rs`:

### Core behavior

1. **Idle limit capping**: Raw times `[1.0, 1.1, 35.0, 35.1, 36.0]`. Test three scenarios in one test (or three closely related tests):
   - `idle_limit: Some(2.0)` → effective `[1.0, 1.1, 3.1, 3.2, 4.1]`, duration `4.1`
   - `idle_limit: Some(1.0)` → effective `[1.0, 1.1, 2.1, 2.2, 3.1]`, duration `3.1`
   - `idle_limit: None` → effective equals raw, duration `36.0`

2. **Empty event list**: `raw_times: &[]`, `idle_limit: Some(2.0)`. Duration `0.0`, len `0`, is_empty `true`.

3. **Single event**: Raw `[5.0]`, `idle_limit: Some(2.0)`. Effective `[5.0]`, duration `5.0`.

4. **Multiple consecutive idle gaps**: Raw `[0.0, 1.0, 20.0, 21.0, 40.0]`, limit `Some(2.0)`. Two gaps capped. Expected effective: `[0.0, 1.0, 3.0, 4.0, 6.0]`, duration `6.0`.

5. **First event at t>0**: Raw `[5.0, 6.0, 7.0]`, limit `Some(2.0)`. First event time 5.0 is preserved (not capped). Expected: `[5.0, 6.0, 7.0]`, duration `7.0`.

### Error cases

6. **Invalid idle limits**: `Some(0.0)` and `Some(-1.0)` both → `TimeMapError::InvalidIdleLimit`. Can be one test with two assertions.

### `event_index_at` tests

7. **Binary search correctness**: Build a TimeMap from test 1 (effective times `[1.0, 1.1, 3.1, 3.2, 4.1]`). Verify:
   - `event_index_at(0.5)` → `None` (before first)
   - `event_index_at(1.0)` → `Some(0)` (exact first)
   - `event_index_at(1.05)` → `Some(0)` (between)
   - `event_index_at(2.0)` → `Some(1)` (between)
   - `event_index_at(4.1)` → `Some(4)` (exact last)
   - `event_index_at(100.0)` → `Some(4)` (past end)
   - Empty map: `event_index_at(0.0)` → `None`
   - Single-event map (`[5.0]`): `event_index_at(4.0)` → `None`, `event_index_at(5.0)` → `Some(0)`

### Insta snapshot

8. Snapshot the effective times for the long_idle scenario (test 1, limit 2.0). Use `insta::assert_debug_snapshot!`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `cargo test -p speedrun-core -- timemap` passes
- [ ] All 8 test cases are implemented and pass
- [ ] Edge cases for empty, single-event, before-first-event, and past-end are covered
- [ ] Error cases for invalid idle limits are tested
- [ ] Insta snapshot captures the effective times
notes:
## Codebase context

Ticket 1.3.1 will have created `timemap.rs` with the full implementation. You are adding tests to this file.

## Snapshotting private fields

The `effective_times` field on `TimeMap` is private. For the insta snapshot test (#14), build a vec from the public accessor:

```rust
let times: Vec<f64> = (0..tm.len())
    .map(|i| tm.effective_time(i).unwrap())
    .collect();
insta::assert_debug_snapshot!(times);
```

Do NOT make `effective_times` public. Use the accessor method.

## insta usage

`insta` is already in dev-dependencies. First run creates snapshot files in `crates/speedrun-core/src/snapshots/`. Run `cargo insta review` to accept (or set `INSTA_UPDATE=always`).

## Verification commands

```bash
cargo test -p speedrun-core -- timemap
cargo clippy -p speedrun-core -- -D warnings
cargo fmt --check
```

title: Epic 1.2 — Asciicast Parser
description:
Parse asciicast v2 and v3 files into an in-memory event list with typed events, eager resize parsing, and strict error handling.

## Context

The parser reads from `impl Read` (wrapped in `BufReader` internally). V2 timestamps are absolute; V3 timestamps are relative intervals normalized to absolute during parsing.

### Key design decisions

- **Strict error handling**: fails on first malformed event line. Lenient/skip mode deferred to Epic 4.1.
- **Eager resize parsing**: `"r"` events with `"COLSxROWS"` data are parsed into `EventData::Resize { cols, rows }` at parse time — no stringly-typed data passed downstream.
- **Monotonicity validation**: V2 timestamps must be non-decreasing, V3 intervals must be non-negative.
- **Markers extracted twice**: present in the events vec (for replay ordering) AND in a separate `markers: Vec<Marker>` (for player navigation API).

### Error types

- `EmptyFile` — zero bytes / no lines
- `InvalidHeader` — first line isn't valid JSON header (with source error)
- `UnsupportedVersion` — version not 2 or 3
- `MissingField` — header missing width/height
- `InvalidEvent` — malformed event line (with line number)
- `InvalidResize` — bad `"COLSxROWS"` format
- `NotUtf8` / `Io` — I/O and encoding errors

## Scope

- `crates/speedrun-core/src/parser.rs` (new)
- Updates to `lib.rs`

## Tickets

- 1.2.1 — Parser types and error handling
- 1.2.2 — Parse implementation
- 1.2.3 — Parser tests
notes:
## Update: Unknown event types are skipped, not errored

Analysis of a real v3 recording revealed event type `"x"` (exit) which is not in the o/i/m/r set. The parser now **skips unknown event types silently** rather than erroring. This ensures real-world recordings parse successfully.

Also: V3 headers use `term.cols`/`term.rows` instead of top-level `width`/`height`. The `RawHeader` serde struct handles both formats.

title: 1.2.1 — Parser types and error handling
description:
Define all parser types, the error enum, and wire the module into lib.rs. No parsing logic yet.

## Cross-cutting conventions

- **Derives**: `Header`: `Debug, Clone, Serialize`. `Event`: `Debug, Clone, Serialize`. `EventType`: `Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize`. `EventData`: `Debug, Clone, Serialize`. `Marker`: `Debug, Clone, Serialize`. `Recording`: `Debug, Serialize`.
- **`impl Read` API**: The public `parse()` function signature accepts `impl Read`, wraps in `BufReader` internally. Declare the signature as a stub (returning `todo!()`) so the API shape is visible.

## Tasks

1. Create `crates/speedrun-core/src/parser.rs` with types:

   ```rust
   #[derive(Debug, Serialize)]
   pub struct Recording {
       pub header: Header,
       pub events: Vec<Event>,
       pub markers: Vec<Marker>,  // Extracted from events for convenience
   }

   #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
   pub struct Header {
       pub version: u8,           // 2 or 3
       pub width: u16,
       pub height: u16,
       pub timestamp: Option<u64>,
       pub idle_time_limit: Option<f64>,
       pub title: Option<String>,
       pub env: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   }

   #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]
   pub enum EventType {
       Output,
       Input,
       Marker,
       Resize,
   }

   #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
   pub struct Event {
       pub time: f64,              // Raw absolute timestamp (seconds)
       pub event_type: EventType,
       pub data: EventData,
   }

   /// Typed event data. Resize is eagerly parsed into structured dimensions
   /// so downstream consumers never parse "COLSxROWS" strings.
   #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
   pub enum EventData {
       Text(String),
       Resize { cols: u16, rows: u16 },
   }

   #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
   pub struct Marker {
       pub time: f64,    // Raw absolute timestamp (effective time from TimeMap)
       pub label: String,
   }
   ```

2. Define `ParseError`:
   ```rust
   #[derive(Debug)]
   pub enum ParseError {
       EmptyFile,
       InvalidHeader { line: String, source: serde_json::Error },
       UnsupportedVersion { version: u64 },
       MissingField { field: &'static str },
       InvalidEvent { line_number: usize, content: String, reason: String },
       InvalidResize { line_number: usize, data: String },
       NotUtf8 { source: std::io::Error },
       Io { source: std::io::Error },
   }
   ```
   Implement `std::fmt::Display` and `std::error::Error` for `ParseError`.

3. Declare a private serde-deserialization struct for the raw header:
   ```rust
   #[derive(Deserialize)]
   struct RawHeader {
       version: u64,              // u64 to detect unsupported versions like 99
       width: Option<u16>,
       height: Option<u16>,
       timestamp: Option<u64>,
       idle_time_limit: Option<f64>,
       title: Option<String>,
       env: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   }
   ```

4. Declare the public API stub:
   ```rust
   pub fn parse(reader: impl std::io::Read) -> Result<Recording, ParseError> {
       todo!()
   }
   ```

5. Add `pub mod parser;` to `lib.rs`. Re-export key types: `pub use parser::{Recording, Header, Event, EventType, EventData, Marker, ParseError, parse};`

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `cargo build -p speedrun-core` passes with zero warnings
- [ ] All types have the specified derives
- [ ] `ParseError` implements `Display` and `Error`
- [ ] `parse()` stub exists with the correct signature
- [ ] `RawHeader` is private (not exported)
- [ ] Types are exported from `lib.rs`
notes:
## Codebase context

**Current state of files you will modify:**

- `crates/speedrun-core/src/lib.rs` contains only: `// speedrun-core library - Terminal session player core engine` (1 line, no modules, no imports).
- `crates/speedrun-core/Cargo.toml` already has `serde.workspace = true` (with derive feature enabled at workspace level) and `serde_json.workspace = true`. No new dependencies needed.
- `parser.rs` does not exist yet — create it.

**Other epics may modify `lib.rs` concurrently** (snapshot.rs and timemap.rs are being built in parallel). Just add your `pub mod parser;` line and re-exports. Do not assume the file is empty or that you own it exclusively.

**Serde usage**: The workspace declares `serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }`, so `#[derive(Serialize)]` works with just `use serde::Serialize;`. For `Deserialize` (used on `RawHeader`): `use serde::Deserialize;`.

**The `todo!()` stub** is intentional — the implementation comes in ticket 1.2.2. The stub lets the types compile and be visible in the API while deferring logic. `cargo test` will not call `parse()` so the `todo!()` will not panic.

## Verification commands

Run these before marking done:
```bash
cargo build -p speedrun-core
cargo clippy -p speedrun-core -- -D warnings
cargo fmt --check
```
## IMPORTANT: V3 header format differs from V2

The real v3 asciicast format nests dimensions under `term`:

**V2 header**: `{"version": 2, "width": 80, "height": 24, ...}`
**V3 header**: `{"version": 3, "term": {"cols": 188, "rows": 50, "type": "xterm-ghostty", ...}, ...}`

The `RawHeader` serde struct must handle both. Recommended approach:

```rust
#[derive(Deserialize)]
struct RawHeader {
    version: u64,
    // V2 fields (top-level)
    width: Option<u16>,
    height: Option<u16>,
    // V3 fields (nested under term)
    term: Option<RawTerm>,
    // Shared
    timestamp: Option<u64>,
    idle_time_limit: Option<f64>,
    title: Option<String>,
    env: Option<serde_json::Value>,
}

#[derive(Deserialize)]
struct RawTerm {
    cols: Option<u16>,
    rows: Option<u16>,
    #[serde(rename = "type")]
    term_type: Option<String>,
    version: Option<String>,
    // ignore theme and other fields
}
```

When converting to public `Header`:
- V2: use `width`/`height` directly
- V3: use `term.cols`/`term.rows`
- Validation: after version check, verify the appropriate fields are present. MissingField error should say "width" (v2) or "term.cols" (v3) depending on version.

title: 1.2.2 — Parse implementation
description:
Implement the `parse()` function: header parsing, event parsing, V3 normalization, resize eager parsing, monotonicity validation.

## Tasks

1. **`parse()` entry point**: Accept `impl Read`, wrap in `BufReader`, read lines, delegate to header and event parsing helpers.

2. **Header parsing**:
   - Read first line. If EOF → `ParseError::EmptyFile`.
   - Binary garbage detection: if the first line contains non-UTF8 bytes, or if the first 32 bytes contain non-printable characters (excluding \t, \n, \r), return an early error (`NotUtf8` or `InvalidHeader`).
   - Deserialize via serde into `RawHeader` (the private struct from 1.2.1). On failure → `InvalidHeader { line, source }`.
   - Validate `version` is 2 or 3 → else `UnsupportedVersion { version }`.
   - Validate `width` is present → else `MissingField { field: "width" }`.
   - Validate `height` is present → else `MissingField { field: "height" }`.
   - Convert `RawHeader` to public `Header` (narrow version from u64 to u8).

3. **Event parsing**:
   - Read subsequent lines. Track line numbers (starting from 1 for the header).
   - Skip empty/whitespace-only lines gracefully.
   - Parse each line as `serde_json::Value`. If not valid JSON → `InvalidEvent { line_number, content, reason: "invalid JSON" }`.
   - Expect a JSON array of exactly 3 elements → else `InvalidEvent` with reason "expected array of 3 elements".
   - Extract timestamp: must be a JSON number, non-null. Convert to f64. If null or non-numeric → `InvalidEvent` with reason "invalid timestamp".
   - Validate timestamp/interval is non-negative → else `InvalidEvent` with reason "negative timestamp".
   - Extract event type string: map `"o"→Output`, `"i"→Input`, `"m"→Marker`, `"r"→Resize`. Unknown type → `InvalidEvent` with reason "unknown event type: X".
   - Extract data: must be a JSON string → else `InvalidEvent`.

4. **V3 timestamp normalization**: If `header.version == 3`, timestamps are relative intervals. Accumulate absolute time:
   ```
   absolute_time = 0.0
   for each event:
       absolute_time += interval
       event.time = absolute_time
   ```
   The first event's interval is relative to t=0.

5. **Monotonicity check (V2 only)**: For version 2, verify each event's timestamp >= the previous event's timestamp. If not → `InvalidEvent { line_number, content, reason: "timestamps must be non-decreasing" }`. (V3 intervals are already validated as non-negative in step 3.)

6. **Resize eager parsing**: If event type is `Resize`, parse the data string:
   - Split on `'x'`. Expect exactly 2 parts.
   - Parse each part as `u16`.
   - On failure → `InvalidResize { line_number, data }`.
   - Store as `EventData::Resize { cols, rows }`.
   - **Note**: the data format is `"COLSxROWS"` (cols first, rows second). The xtwinops escape sequence uses the opposite order (`rows;cols`) — but that's the indexer's concern, not the parser's. The parser stores the parsed values by name.

7. **Marker extraction**: If event type is `Marker`, push `Marker { time, label: data_string }` to the markers vec. The event also goes into the events vec with `EventData::Text(label)`.

8. **All other events**: Store `EventData::Text(data_string)`.

9. Return `Recording { header, events, markers }`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `parse()` works end-to-end for all 8 valid testdata files (verify in next ticket)
- [ ] V3 timestamps are normalized to absolute
- [ ] Resize data is eagerly parsed into `EventData::Resize { cols, rows }`
- [ ] Markers appear in both `events` vec and `markers` vec
- [ ] V2 non-monotonic timestamps produce `InvalidEvent`
- [ ] V3 negative intervals produce `InvalidEvent`
- [ ] Empty/whitespace-only lines are skipped
- [ ] Line numbers in errors are correct (1-indexed, header is line 1)
- [ ] Strict mode: first malformed event line stops parsing and returns error
notes:
## Codebase context

Ticket 1.2.1 will have already created `parser.rs` with all types, `ParseError`, `RawHeader`, and a `parse()` stub returning `todo!()`. You are replacing the `todo!()` with the real implementation.

## UTF-8 and binary garbage handling — clarification

`BufReader::lines()` returns `io::Result<String>`. If the underlying bytes are not valid UTF-8, `lines()` returns `Err(io::Error { kind: InvalidData, ... })`. This means:

- **You do NOT need to pre-read raw bytes** for binary garbage detection. Just attempt to read the first line via `lines().next()`. If it returns `Err(e)` with `e.kind() == ErrorKind::InvalidData`, map it to `ParseError::NotUtf8 { source: e }`.
- If it returns `Ok(line)`, proceed with JSON parsing.
- The `binary_garbage.cast` test file will trigger this path naturally.

So the actual flow is:
```rust
let reader = BufReader::new(reader);
let mut lines = reader.lines();
let first_line = match lines.next() {
    None => return Err(ParseError::EmptyFile),
    Some(Err(e)) => /* check e.kind() for InvalidData → NotUtf8, else Io */,
    Some(Ok(line)) => line,
};
// Now parse `first_line` as header JSON...
```

## Verification commands

After implementing, smoke-test with at least one file:
```bash
cargo build -p speedrun-core
cargo clippy -p speedrun-core -- -D warnings
cargo fmt --check
# Quick smoke test (will be fully tested in 1.2.3):
cargo test -p speedrun-core
```
## IMPORTANT: Two changes from real-world recording analysis

### 1. V3 header parsing

V3 headers nest dimensions under `term`:
```json
{"version": 3, "term": {"cols": 188, "rows": 50, "type": "xterm-ghostty"}, "timestamp": 1772729753, "env": {"SHELL": "/bin/zsh"}}
```

When parsing the header, after detecting version:
- **V2**: read `width` and `height` from top level
- **V3**: read `term.cols` and `term.rows` from nested `term` object

Both produce the same `Header` struct (which has `width` and `height` fields).

### 2. Unknown event types are SKIPPED, not errored

Real-world v3 recordings contain event types like `"x"` (exit) that our parser does not handle. The parser should **skip unknown event types silently** rather than returning `InvalidEvent`. This is a pragmatic choice — real recordings must parse successfully.

Change from the original plan:
- **Before**: Unknown event type → `InvalidEvent` with reason "unknown event type: X"
- **After**: Unknown event type → skip the event, do not add to events vec, continue parsing

The synthetic "unknown event type" test in 1.2.3 should verify the event is skipped and parsing continues (not that it errors).

title: 1.2.3 — Parser tests
description:
Tests for the asciicast parser: valid files, invalid files, edge cases, and insta snapshot.

## Cross-cutting conventions

- **Test fixture helper**: Define inside the test module:
  ```rust
  fn testdata_path(name: &str) -> std::path::PathBuf {
      let mut p = std::path::PathBuf::from(env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR"));
      p.push("../../testdata");
      p.push(name);
      p
  }
  ```
- **File reading pattern**: `let file = std::fs::File::open(testdata_path("minimal_v2.cast")).unwrap();`

## Valid file tests

1. `minimal_v2.cast` — Verify: 4 events, all `EventType::Output`, timestamps `[0.5, 1.2, 2.0, 2.1]`, all `EventData::Text`, header 80x24 v2, 0 markers.

2. `minimal_v3.cast` — Verify: 4 events, all Output, timestamps normalized to absolute `[0.5, 1.2, 2.0, 2.9]` (raw intervals in file: 0.5, 0.7, 0.8, 0.9).

3. `empty.cast` — Verify: 0 events, 0 markers, header valid (80x24, v2).

4. `with_markers.cast` — Verify: 8 events total (6 Output + 2 Marker). `recording.markers` has 2 entries with labels `"chapter-1"` and `"chapter-2"` at times 3.0 and 7.0. Marker events also present in `events` vec.

5. `with_resize.cast` — Verify: 6 events. Event at index 2 has `EventType::Resize` with `EventData::Resize { cols: 120, rows: 40 }`.

6. **Remaining valid files**: One test that verifies `long_idle.cast`, `alternate_buffer.cast`, and `real_session.cast` all parse successfully. Spot-check: `long_idle.cast` header has `idle_time_limit == Some(2.0)` with 5 events; `real_session.cast` has 30 events with `title == Some("Development Session")`; `alternate_buffer.cast` has 9 events.

## Invalid file tests

7. **All invalid files**: Test each invalid file produces the expected error variant:
   - `invalid/empty_file.cast` → `ParseError::EmptyFile`
   - `invalid/no_header.cast` → `ParseError::InvalidHeader`
   - `invalid/bad_json.cast` → `ParseError::InvalidHeader` (serde error in source)
   - `invalid/bad_version.cast` → `ParseError::UnsupportedVersion { version: 99 }`
   - `invalid/missing_fields.cast` → `ParseError::MissingField`
   - `invalid/bad_event.cast` → `ParseError::InvalidEvent` with `line_number: 2` (first bad line: `[0.5, "o"]`). Strict mode: parsing stops here.
   - `invalid/binary_garbage.cast` → error on first line (either `NotUtf8` or `InvalidHeader`)
   - `invalid/truncated.cast` → `ParseError::InvalidEvent` for the truncated line

   These can be individual tests or one test with sub-assertions — agent's choice.

## Synthetic edge case tests (in-memory, not from files)

8. **V2 non-monotonic timestamps**: Construct input with events at `[1.0, 3.0, 2.0]`. Expect `InvalidEvent` with reason containing "non-decreasing".

9. **V3 negative interval**: Construct v3 input with intervals `[0.5, -0.1, 0.3]`. Expect `InvalidEvent` with reason containing "negative".

10. **Unknown event type**: Construct input with event `[1.0, "x", "data"]`. Expect `InvalidEvent` with reason containing "unknown event type".

11. **Resize bad format**: Test at least 2 malformed resize data strings (e.g., `"120"` missing separator, `"abcxdef"` non-numeric). Both should produce `InvalidResize`.

## Insta snapshot test

12. Parse `minimal_v2.cast`, snapshot the full `Recording` struct with `insta::assert_debug_snapshot!`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `cargo test -p speedrun-core -- parser` passes
- [ ] All valid testdata files parse successfully
- [ ] All invalid testdata files produce the expected error variant
- [ ] Synthetic edge cases for monotonicity, negative interval, unknown type, and bad resize are covered
- [ ] Insta snapshot captures the full Recording output
notes:
## Codebase context

Tickets 1.2.1 and 1.2.2 will have created `parser.rs` with the full implementation. You are adding tests to this file.

## insta usage

`insta` is already in dev-dependencies. Usage:
```rust
use insta::assert_debug_snapshot;

#[test]
fn test_parse_minimal_v2_snapshot() {
    let file = std::fs::File::open(testdata_path("minimal_v2.cast")).unwrap();
    let recording = parse(file).unwrap();
    assert_debug_snapshot!(recording);
}
```

On first run, this creates a snapshot file in `crates/speedrun-core/src/snapshots/`. Run `cargo insta review` to accept it (or it auto-accepts if `INSTA_UPDATE=always` is set).

## testdata_path helper

Define this **inside** the `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block:
```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    fn testdata_path(name: &str) -> std::path::PathBuf {
        let mut p = std::path::PathBuf::from(env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR"));
        p.push("../../testdata");
        p.push(name);
        p
    }

    // tests go here...
}
```

## Verification commands

```bash
cargo test -p speedrun-core -- parser
cargo clippy -p speedrun-core -- -D warnings
cargo fmt --check
```
## IMPORTANT: Updated test expectations

### `minimal_v3.cast` changed

The file now uses real v3 header format with nested `term`:
```json
{"version": 3, "term": {"cols": 80, "rows": 24}}
```
Test expectations remain the same (4 events, timestamps [0.5, 1.2, 2.0, 2.9]) but header parsing now goes through the `term.cols`/`term.rows` path.

### `real_session.cast` replaced with a real recording

The old synthetic `real_session.cast` (v2, 30 events, fake dev session) has been replaced with an actual asciinema recording. New properties:
- **Version 3** (relative timestamps)
- **188x50 terminal** (large ghostty terminal)
- **115 events**: 114 output (`"o"`) + 1 exit (`"x"`)
- **No `title` field**, no `idle_time_limit`
- **Has `timestamp`**: 1772729753
- **Contains alternate buffer** escape sequences (a TUI app runs and exits)
- **The `"x"` event is an unknown type** — the parser should skip it

Updated test for remaining valid files (test #6): verify `real_session.cast` parses successfully as v3, header has `width == 188` and `height == 50`, at least 100 events (114 output events after skipping the `"x"`), and has `timestamp == Some(1772729753)`.

### "Unknown event type" synthetic test updated

Test #10 now verifies that unknown event types are **skipped** (not errored). Construct an input with a valid `"o"` event, then a `"x"` event, then another `"o"` event. Verify the recording has 2 events (both output), not 3.
